channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Brussels hosts 'United for Palestine' concert as Eurovision alternative
item.description: Several NGOs are organising a concert on Tuesday in response to calls for a Eurovision boycott. "We refuse to accept double standards."

item.title: Man injured after being violently robbed in Saint-Gilles
item.description: A man was injured in a violent robbery in the Brussels municipality of Saint-Gilles, the local police zone in the Brussels-Midi area.

item.title: Brussels mayors consider taking 'discriminatory' police zone merger to court
item.description: "In the rest of the country, police zones are encouraged to merge. But that happens on a voluntary basis. In Brussels, this merger is being imposed."

item.title: Shootings, floods or terrorist attacks: Are Brussels' EU institutions ready for a crisis?
item.description: Brussels' regular chain of command is complex enough. But should an emergency happen at the EU institutions, there are "some extra layers of complication".

item.title: Only one in three Brussels bars still serve small bowl of nuts with beer
item.description: The tradition of getting a little bowl of free peanuts when ordering an alcoholic drink – particularly a strong beer – in Brussels' bars is slowly disappearing.

item.title: Under the radar: Where can you savour Georgian food in Brussels?
item.description: While the Georgian community in Belgium is relatively small, the country's celebrated cuisine is beginning to make its mark.

item.title: Person injured in violent theft in Brussels
item.description: A security perimeter was set up in Fabrieksstraat following a violent robbery that left one injured. Police are now searching for the suspect.

item.title: Savings plan announced after report exposes lavish spending by Dutch-speaking Brussels MPs
item.description: The Council of the Flemish Community Commission has approved a €284,000 savings plan after an investigation by La Libre.

item.title: Union Saint-Gilloise asks fans to donate old mobile phones at Sunday's game
item.description: This Sunday, the Belgian champions will compete with other clubs in a phone-recycling competition organised by the non-profit organisation Recupel.

item.title: Brussels' bilingual babies: How easy is it to raise multilingual children?
item.description: The Brussels Times speaks to parents in the Belgian capital to find out how they're raising the next generation of polyglots.

item.title: Brussels celebrates Iris Day: What is happening this weekend?
item.description: Whether you're looking for a huge open-air DJ party, a new way to discover your local commune or more info about the EU's history, Iris Day has it all.

item.title: Brussels Government divided over 'dangerous' Louise roundabout redevelopment
item.description: "Returning to an unsafe situation is not an option," said Brussels Mobility Minister Elke Van den Brandt (Groen).

item.title: Inside Belgium's wild beer tradition: Sipping 'Brussels champagne' at the Toer de Geuze
item.description: The Toer de Geuze returns this weekend, giving beer lovers the chance to step inside some of Belgium’s most iconic lambic breweries

item.title: Streets left in 'deplorable state' as rubbish collections missed in several Brussels communes
item.description: Numerous collection rounds were missed on Wednesday evening and Thursday due to staff shortages.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 8 to 10 May
item.description: This is the big Brussels celebration weekend, with free live music and events dotted across the city - your only problem will be to choose what you go to.

item.title: New rainbow pedestrian crossings painted in Brussels last night
item.description: Just over a week before Brussels Pride on 16 May, Brussels has been adorned with new rainbow flag-painted crossings.

item.title: Flemish nationalists call on Vivaqua CEO to resign over unpaid water bills in Brussels
item.description: Regional Brussels MP Mathias Vanden Borre (N-VA) has raised concerns about the sharp rise in unpaid bills at Vivaqua and is calling for new management.

item.title: Traffic disruptions expected on Brussels outer ring road from 18 May
item.description: The viaduct of Groot-Bijgaarden on the Brussels outer ring will undergo repair work on a bridge joint from Monday, 18 May to Sunday, 7 June.

item.title: Monument for former Belgian cyclist Eddy Merckx vandalised
item.description: The Brussels municipality was alerted on Wednesday morning by local residents that the head of the sculpture had been torn off.

item.title: Molenbeek honours 40 young people for response to hooligans' racist rampage
item.description: A group of young people organised a series of initiatives in response to a racially-motivated rampage through Molenbeek by Club Brugge fans one year ago.
